goal: Give Alex a minimal pick-and-place path in 30 minutes by retargeting the existing Pick & Place sample to his cube position — no RL, no reward functions, no training loops.
thoughts: The canned Pick & Place sample exists — point Alex at it rather than authoring motion planning. list_scene_templates + load_scene_template is the beginner path. scene_summary confirms his cube + UR5e are named/positioned as the sample expects. If his URDF is non-standard, one-line warn that retargeting needs joint remapping. Never mention IsaacLab/ONNX/RL.
code:
# 4 steps. No training.

# 1. Find the Pick & Place template
list_scene_templates(category="manipulation")

# 2. Load it into the stage
load_scene_template(
    template_id="<PICK_AND_PLACE_TEMPLATE_ID>",
    target_stage="<CURRENT_STAGE>"
)

# 3. Confirm your robot + cube are where the sample expects
scene_summary(stage_path="<CURRENT_STAGE>")

# 4. Edit the target pose in the sample's config to match your cube (x,y,z).
#    Hit Play.

# If the arm moves but misses: adjust the TCP offset or the target-pose z-value
# by the cube half-height. One number, one try.
#
# If your URDF isn't a Franka or UR, the sample assumes one of those — you'll
# need to remap the joint names in its config. That's a small edit, not a rewrite.
failure_modes: Mentioning reward functions / RL / IsaacLab — tab closed
Authoring motion planning from scratch when the sample exists
Forgetting the TCP offset tip for the 'arm misses cube' case
Sending a multi-file motion-planner config template — too much
